diagrams.title: Pascal's Triangle (Rows 0-6)
diagrams.description: Visual representation of binomial coefficients arranged in triangular form
diagrams.purpose: Helps visualize Pascal's Identity and symmetry property. Each number is sum of two numbers above it.

diagrams.title: Binomial Expansion Term Structure
diagrams.description: Shows how terms are arranged in binomial expansion (x+y)^n
elements: General term T(k+1) = C(n,k) × a^(n-k) × b^k when expansion is (a+b)^n
diagrams.purpose: Shows position numbering, general term formula, and how coefficients relate to binomial coefficients.

diagrams.title: Symmetry in Binomial Coefficients
diagrams.description: Demonstrates that C(n,k) = C(n,n-k) using row 5 as example
elements: Visual symmetry axis between positions 2 and 3
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the symmetry property: choosing k items equals choosing n-k items to exclude.

diagrams.title: Combinatorial Interpretation of C(n,k)
diagrams.description: Visual representation of selecting k elements from n-element set
elements: Set of n elements shown as n distinct circles
Highlighting k selected elements (filled) and n-k unselected (empty)
Different selection arrangements shown for small n
Shows why order doesn't matter in combinations
diagrams.purpose: Reinforces the fundamental meaning of binomial coefficient as number of ways to choose without regard to order.
